metadata.title: Why typing one word can multiply your API bill 100×
metadata.topic: Reasoning-budget keywords as a hidden cost parameter
metadata.purpose: Claude Code maps a fixed phrase ladder ("think" < "think hard" < "think harder" < "ultrathink") to reasoning-budget multipliers; each level accumulates thousands of hidden inference tokens billed at full rate but never shown in the transcript.
metadata.clock: narration (Kokoro am_onyx) — master clock; conform each beat to measured audio
metadata.exclusions: extended-thinking API config; model internals/benchmark deltas; plan behavior
metadata.visual_object: Two token receipts — visible input/output bars roughly equal between runs, a hidden reasoning bar dwarfing them under "ultrathink," dollar amounts embedded.
metadata.hook: "Ultrathink" isn't a style choice — it silently allocates thousands of hidden reasoning tokens your dashboard never itemizes.

beats.narration_text: The same architectural analysis costs $0.003 with "think" and $0.34 with "ultrathink" — same codebase, same question, near-identical visible output.
beats.scaffold_note: from Key case — cold open on the concrete instance

beats.narration_text: If cost is metered by visible input and output tokens, how does a single English word multiply the bill 100× without changing what the developer sees?
"Ultrathink" isn't a style choice — it silently allocates thousands of hidden reasoning tokens your dashboard never itemizes.

Claude Code maps a fixed phrase ladder ("think" < "think hard" < "think harder" < "ultrathink") to reasoning-budget multipliers; each level accumulates thousands of hidden inference tokens billed at full rate but never shown in the transcript.
beats.visual: Two token receipts — visible input/output bars roughly equal between runs, a hidden reasoning bar dwarfing them under "ultrathink," dollar amounts embedded.
beats.scaffold_note: the resolving mechanism; orbits the visual object

beats.narration_text: "ultrathink about the best schema…" → 820 in / 580 out / ~21,000 hidden reasoning → $0.34. Same query with "think" → ~180 reasoning → $0.003.

Claude Code maps a fixed phrase ladder ("think" < "think hard" < "think harder" < "ultrathink") to reasoning-budget multipliers; each level accumulates thousands of hidden inference tokens billed at full rate but never shown in the transcript.
